title: Course Milestone: Phase J Wrap-up
concept: Recap of kNN, Naive Bayes, SVMs, Trees, Ensembles, and Time Series as the completion of our expanded core.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main focus of Directional Accuracy?

options.text: Predicting whether a value will move Up or Down compared to the previous period.
Calculating the exact decimal value of a forecast.
Checking if the seasonality cycle is exactly 7 days.

exercises.explain: Correct! Directional accuracy measures the percentage of times we correctly predict the trend direction.

cards.title: The Grand Tour complete
cards.body: Congratulations! You have completed our expanded machine learning core. From finding nearest neighbors to splitting decisions, drawing support vector boundaries, building ensemble boosting teams, and forecasting into the future, you now have a complete classic ML toolbox.

cards.title: What did we learn?
cards.body:
Let's look back at the key concepts of this phase:
• kNN: Voting using closest neighbors.
• Naive Bayes: Independent probability clues.
• SVMs: Drawing wide separation streets.
• Trees & Ensembles: Random Forests (bagging) and sequential corrections (boosting).
• Time Series: Lags, ARIMA, and walk-forward validation.

cards.title: A builder's toolbox
cards.body: A builder doesn't use a hammer for every job. They use a tape measure (metrics), a saw (decision trees), glue (ensembles), and a blueprint (time series) to construct a stable house. You now know which tool to pull from your belt for any data problem.

cards.title: Running a smart farm
cards.body:
Imagine running a modern agricultural business:
• Classify soil type? Use kNN or SVMs.
• Predict email spam? Use Naive Bayes.
• Predict crop yields? Use Random Forests or Boosting.
• Forecast tomorrow's temperature? Use Time Series models with lag features, tested with a walk-forward split.

cards.title: Simpler is often better
cards.body:
Myth: Now that you know ensembles and ARIMA, you should never use simple decision trees or naive baselines.
Reality: Simple models are easier to explain, faster to run, less prone to overfitting, and serve as crucial baselines. Always start simple and only add complexity if it beats your benchmark!

cards.title: Static vs. Sequential Models
cards.body: How classic machine learning approaches data ordering:

left.text:
Assume rows are independent.
Examples: SVMs, Trees, kNN.
Best for classification & standard regression.

right.text:
Row order matters chronologically.
Examples: ARIMA, Lag features.
Best for time series forecasting.

exercises.prompt: Which algorithm is best suited for email spam detection by calculating probabilities from independent keyword clues?
exercises.explain: Exactly! Naive Bayes uses independent probabilities, making it extremely effective for fast text classification like spam detection.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Random Forests use boosting to train trees sequentially, correcting errors one by one.
exercises.explain: False. Random Forests use bagging (parallel training of trees). Boosting (like AdaBoost) trains trees sequentially.

exercises.sentence: While standard models assume rows are independent, time series models must respect the chronological ___ of the data.
exercises.explain: Time series data relies heavily on the chronological order, meaning we cannot shuffle the rows randomly.

exercises.prompt: Which model choice shows a correct understanding of ML tool selection?

options.text: Using an ARIMA model with walk-forward validation to forecast monthly ice cream sales.
Shuffling weather history to train a standard decision tree for predicting next week's temperature.
Using Naive Bayes to draw a high-dimensional support vector margin for house pricing.

exercises.explain: Using ARIMA (which models time dependencies) alongside walk-forward validation (which prevents future leakage) is the correct approach for time series forecasting.

exercises.prompt: You are hired as the Lead AI Engineer at a shipping port. You need to build a system that: 1. Classifies if containers contain fragile cargo, and 2. Forecasts the daily arrival rate of ships. Which combination of techniques is correct?

options.text: Use a Decision Tree/Random Forest for fragile cargo classification, and an ARIMA/Time-Series model tested via Walk-Forward validation for ship arrivals.
Use K-Fold cross-validation to shuffle ship arrivals, and use Naive Bayes to forecast the exact arrival numbers.
Use ARIMA to classify cargo, and standard kNN without time-awareness to forecast ship arrivals.
Use a simple coin-toss generator to replace the entire port operations without any validation.

exercises.explain: Correct! Decision Trees/Random Forests are excellent for static tabular classification (fragile vs. normal), while ARIMA combined with Walk-Forward validation is the correct framework for time series forecasting.
